channel.title: TABI 窶・Discover Japan
channel.description: Your guide to the real Japan — travel, culture, and the things worth bringing home.

item.title: The Best Time to Visit Japan: An Honest Month-by-Month Guide
item.description: Spring cherry blossoms and autumn foliage are spectacular. They are also the two most crowded periods of the year. Here is what every month actually offers.

item.title: The Japanese Breakfast: What It Is and Why It Will Change Your Morning
item.description: A traditional Japanese breakfast is not a quick meal. It is a composed sequence of small things done well, and eating it once will make every hotel breakfast you eat for the rest of your life feel like a concession.

item.title: Taking a Taxi in Japan: What to Know Before You Get In
item.description: Japanese taxis are expensive, exceptionally clean, and almost entirely driven by people who know exactly where they are going. The main things to know are not about navigation.

item.title: Kyoto in Three Days: What to Actually See and What to Skip
item.description: Kyoto has over 1,600 temples and shrines. You will not see them all. Here is how to choose the ones that matter 窶・and what makes them worth choosing over the others.

item.title: Matsumoto: The Castle Town That Gets Everything Right
item.description: Two and a half hours from Tokyo, a city with one of Japan's three great original castles, a mountain backdrop, an excellent food scene, and a fraction of Kyoto's visitor numbers. There is no explanation for why more people don't come here.

item.title: The Rules of Gift Giving in Japan: What to Give, How to Give It, When Not To
item.description: Gift giving in Japan is governed by a set of conventions that are invisible until you get them wrong. Here is what they are.

item.title: How to Use Chopsticks in Japan 窶・and What Never to Do
item.description: Using chopsticks correctly in Japan is less about technique and more about knowing the four things that are genuinely offensive. The technique is optional. The four things are not.

item.title: Tattoos and Onsen in Japan: What the Policy Actually Is
item.description: Most onsen ban tattoos. Fewer than you'd think actually enforce it. Here is how to navigate it honestly.

item.title: How to Order and Drink Sake in Japan Without Getting It Wrong
item.description: Sake is Japan's most misunderstood drink. Most people drink it too warm, in the wrong glass, with the wrong food. This is how to do it correctly.

item.title: Meishi: The Japanese Business Card Ritual and Why It Matters
item.description: In Japan, the business card is not a piece of paper. It is a physical representation of the person. Treating it as stationary is the fastest way to start a professional relationship poorly.

item.title: When to Take Your Shoes Off in Japan: The Complete Guide
item.description: The shoe rule in Japan is not arbitrary. It reflects a clear line between outside space and inside space that structures daily life. Here is where that line is drawn.

item.title: How to Behave in Public in Japan: The Unwritten Rules
item.description: Japanese public spaces operate on a set of unspoken rules that make them exceptionally pleasant. Most visitors don遯ｶ蜀ｲ know them. All of them are learnable in ten minutes.

item.title: Why You Should Never Tip in Japan 遯ｶ繝ｻand What to Do Instead
item.description: In Japan, the service is built into the price. Adding a tip doesn遯ｶ蜀ｲ reward the server 遯ｶ繝ｻit creates an awkward situation for both of you.

item.title: The Unwritten Rules of Japanese Trains 遯ｶ繝ｻRead This Before You Board
item.description: Japan遯ｶ蜀ｱ trains run on time to the minute and operate on a set of social rules that nobody posts on a sign. Here遯ｶ蜀ｱ what they are.

item.title: How to Visit a Japanese Temple or Shrine Without Being Accidentally Rude
item.description: Temples and shrines have different rules, different purposes, and different gods. Getting them confused is the starting mistake. Everything else follows from there.

item.title: The Tokyo Subway: How to Navigate It Without Getting Lost
item.description: Tokyo遯ｶ蜀ｱ train network is the most complex urban rail system in the world. It is also, once you understand the basics, extremely easy to use.

item.title: What You Can and Cannot Photograph in Japan
item.description: Japan is exceptionally photogenic. It also has specific places and situations where photography is restricted, disrespectful, or now actively prohibited. Knowing the difference matters.

item.title: The Only Japanese Phrases You Actually Need
item.description: Japanese is difficult. A handful of phrases isn遯ｶ蜀ｲ. The ten expressions below will handle the majority of situations a visitor encounters, and they take about 20 minutes to learn.

item.title: Japan Still Runs on Cash 遯ｶ繝ｻHere遯ｶ蜀ｱ How Much to Bring and Where to Get It
item.description: Cards are increasingly accepted in Japan, but 遯ｶ莠冢creasingly遯ｶ繝ｻis not the same as 遯ｶ閻鋲liably.遯ｶ繝ｻPlan for cash and treat the card acceptance as a bonus.

item.title: SIM Card vs Pocket Wi-Fi in Japan: What Nobody Tells You Before You Choose
item.description: Both work. One is significantly more convenient. The answer has changed in the last three years and most travel articles haven遯ｶ蜀ｲ caught up.
